questions.stem: Which term is best described as follows: the physical objects and artifacts that members of a society create and use?

choices.label: A
choices.text: a counterculture

choices.label: B
choices.text: material culture

choices.label: C
choices.text: a moral panic

choices.label: D
choices.text: primary deviance

questions.explanation: This description matches material culture, which is the physical objects and artifacts that members of a society create and use. The other options name different concepts that do not fit this description.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Which of the following best describes ethnocentrism?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Patterns or traits found in all known human cultures.

choices.label: B
choices.text: A culture's shared standards of what is good, desirable, and worthy.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Widespread and exaggerated public fear that some group or behavior threatens society.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The tendency to evaluate other cultures by the standards of one's own culture, seen as superior.

questions.explanation: Ethnocentrism is best described as the tendency to evaluate other cultures by the standards of one's own culture, seen as superior. The other options describe different concepts.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Which of the following is the best example of a subculture?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A community of skateboarders with their own slang, dress, and unspoken etiquette.

choices.label: B
choices.text: A person turning to theft after finding no lawful path to the wealth society prizes.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Sweeping public alarm that a new video game is corrupting the nation's youth.

choices.label: D
choices.text: A large coordinated campaign pressing lawmakers for new environmental protections.

questions.explanation: An example of a subculture is: a community of skateboarders with their own slang, dress, and unspoken etiquette. In general, a subculture refers to a group within a society that shares distinctive norms and values while sharing the larger culture. The other options illustrate different concepts.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Which term is best described as follows: the rules and expectations that guide the behavior of members of a society?

choices.label: B
choices.text: mores

choices.label: C
choices.text: values

choices.label: D
choices.text: norms

questions.explanation: This description matches norms, which is the rules and expectations that guide the behavior of members of a society. The other options name different concepts that do not fit this description.

questions.stem: Which of the following best describes a taboo?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Cultural change that results when two cultures come into continuous firsthand contact.

choices.label: B
choices.text: An organized, sustained effort by many people to promote or resist social change.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Deviance that follows after a person adopts a deviant identity in response to labeling.

choices.label: D
choices.text: A norm so deeply held that its violation provokes extreme disgust or revulsion.

questions.explanation: A taboo is best described as a norm so deeply held that its violation provokes extreme disgust or revulsion. The other options describe different concepts.

questions.stem: Country X has a Gini coefficient of 0.28 for income, while Country Y has a Gini coefficient of 0.55. Which statement is best supported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A Gini coefficient cannot be used to compare countries.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The two countries have identical income distributions.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Income is distributed more unequally in Country X than in Country Y.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Income is distributed more unequally in Country Y than in Country X.

questions.explanation: The Gini coefficient rises from 0 (perfect equality) toward 1 (maximum inequality), so 0.55 indicates greater income inequality than 0.28.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: An experimenter recruits volunteers and then flips a coin to place each one into either a caffeine group or a no-caffeine group. What does the coin flip accomplish?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It removes any need for a control group.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Random assignment, which helps make the groups comparable before the treatment.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Random sampling, which makes the volunteers represent all people.

choices.label: D
choices.text: It guarantees that the caffeine group will perform better.

questions.explanation: Assigning existing participants to conditions by chance is random assignment, which balances the groups. It is not random sampling, which concerns how participants are drawn from a population.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

choices.label: C

questions.stem: Which of the following is the best example of social facilitation?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A skilled cyclist riding faster when other people are watching.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Crediting a good grade to talent but blaming a bad grade on an unfair test.

choices.label: C
choices.text: A teenager trying out different roles and values to arrive at a personal identity.

choices.label: D
choices.text: An infant who is naturally calm and adapts easily to new situations.

questions.explanation: An example of social facilitation is: a skilled cyclist riding faster when other people are watching. In general, social facilitation refers to improved performance on easy or well-learned tasks in the presence of others. The other options illustrate different concepts.
